was a [line infantry](/wiki/Line_infantry "Line infantry") [regiment](/wiki/Regiment "Regiment") of the [British Army](/wiki/British_Army "British Army"), first raised in 1685 as **Sir William Clifton's Regiment of Foot** and later renamed the **15th Regiment of Foot**. It saw service for three centuries, before eventually being amalgamated with the [West Yorkshire Regiment (Prince of Wales's Own)](/wiki/West_Yorkshire_Regiment "West Yorkshire Regiment") in 1958, to form the [Prince of Wales's Own Regiment of Yorkshire](/wiki/Prince_of_Wales%27s_Own_Regiment_of_Yorkshire "Prince of Wales's Own Regiment of Yorkshire"). Subsequently, the regiment amalgamated with the [Green Howards](/wiki/Green_Howards "Green Howards") and the [Duke of Wellington's Regiment (West Riding)](/wiki/Duke_of_Wellington%27s_Regiment "Duke of Wellington's Regiment") to form the [Yorkshire Regiment (14th/15th, 19th and 33rd/76th Foot)](/wiki/Yorkshire_Regiment "Yorkshire Regiment") on 6 June 2006.

From 1895 the battalion was stationed in [British India](/wiki/British_Raj "British Raj"), where they had various postings, including at [Belgaum](/wiki/Belgaum "Belgaum") and [Fort St. George](/wiki/Fort_St._George,_India "Fort St. George, India") in [Madras Presidency](/wiki/Madras_Presidency "Madras Presidency") until late 1902 when it was posted to [Shwebo](/wiki/Shwebo "Shwebo") in Burma.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

Military unit

The **East Yorkshire Regiment** was a [line infantry](/wiki/Line_infantry "Line infantry") [regiment](/wiki/Regiment "Regiment") of the [British Army](/wiki/British_Army "British Army"), first raised in 1685 as **Sir William Clifton's Regiment of Foot** and later renamed the **15th Regiment of Foot**. It saw service for three centuries, before eventually being amalgamated with the [West Yorkshire Regiment (Prince of Wales's Own)](/wiki/West_Yorkshire_Regiment "West Yorkshire Regiment") in 1958, to form the [Prince of Wales's Own Regiment of Yorkshire](/wiki/Prince_of_Wales%27s_Own_Regiment_of_Yorkshire "Prince of Wales's Own Regiment of Yorkshire"). Subsequently, the regiment amalgamated with the [Green Howards](/wiki/Green_Howards "Green Howards") and the [Duke of Wellington's Regiment (West Riding)](/wiki/Duke_of_Wellington%27s_Regiment "Duke of Wellington's Regiment") to form the [Yorkshire Regiment (14th/15th, 19th and 33rd/76th Foot)](/wiki/Yorkshire_Regiment "Yorkshire Regiment") on 6 June 2006.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Yorkshire_Regiment&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Early wars

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Yorkshire_Regiment&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early wars")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d2/Soldier_of_15th_regiment_1742.jpg/250px-Soldier_of_15th_regiment_1742.jpg)](/wiki/File:Soldier_of_15th_regiment_1742.jpg)

Soldier of 15th regiment, 1742

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/74/John_Theophilus_Rawdon-Hastings_by_John_Trotter_%28fl_1756-1792_Dublin%29.jpg/250px-John_Theophilus_Rawdon-Hastings_by_John_Trotter_%28fl_1756-1792_Dublin%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:John_Theophilus_Rawdon-Hastings_by_John_Trotter_(fl_1756-1792_Dublin).jpg)

John Theophilus Rawdon-Hastings, 2nd son of the [1st Earl of Moira](/wiki/John_Rawdon,_1st_Earl_of_Moira "John Rawdon, 1st Earl of Moira"), in the uniform of the 15th Regiment of Foot (1776) with a flintlock gun.

Raised in 1685 in Nottingham by [Sir William Clifton, 3rd Baronet](/wiki/Sir_William_Clifton,_3rd_Baronet "Sir William Clifton, 3rd Baronet"),[[3]](#cite_note-Chant2013-3) the regiment was originally, like many British infantry regiments, known by the name of its current [Colonel](/wiki/Colonel "Colonel").[[4]](#cite_note-regiments-4) It took part in the [Battle of Killiecrankie](/wiki/Battle_of_Killiecrankie "Battle of Killiecrankie") in July 1689[[5]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon20116-5) and the [Battle of Cromdale](/wiki/Battle_of_Cromdale "Battle of Cromdale") in April 1690 during the [Jacobite rising of 1689 to 1692](/wiki/Jacobite_risings "Jacobite risings").[[5]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon20116-5)

The regiment embarked for [Flanders](/wiki/Flanders "Flanders") in spring 1694 for service in the [Nine Years' War](/wiki/Nine_Years%27_War "Nine Years' War") and took part in the capture of [Huy](/wiki/Huy "Huy") in autumn 1694,[[6]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201110-6) the attack of [Fort Knokke](/wiki/Fort_Knokke "Fort Knokke") in June 1695[[6]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201110-6) and the [Siege of Namur](/wiki/Siege_of_Namur_(1695) "Siege of Namur (1695)") in summer 1695[[7]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201112-7) before returning home in 1697.[[8]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201114-8)

The regiment was sent to [Holland](/wiki/Holland "Holland") in 1701 for service in the [War of the Spanish Succession](/wiki/War_of_the_Spanish_Succession "War of the Spanish Succession") and fought at the siege of [Kaiserswerth](/wiki/D%C3%BCsseldorf-Kaiserswerth "Düsseldorf-Kaiserswerth") in 1702,[[9]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201115-9) the siege of [Venlo](/wiki/Venlo "Venlo") later that year[[10]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201116-10) and the [Battle of Blenheim](/wiki/Battle_of_Blenheim "Battle of Blenheim") in August 1704.[[11]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201119-11) It went on to fight at the [Battle of Ramillies](/wiki/Battle_of_Ramillies "Battle of Ramillies") in May 1706,[[12]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201122-12) the [Battle of Oudenarde](/wiki/Battle_of_Oudenarde "Battle of Oudenarde") in July 1708[[13]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201124-13) and the [Battle of Malplaquet](/wiki/Battle_of_Malplaquet "Battle of Malplaquet") in September 1709.[[14]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201126-14) It returned to England in 1714.[[15]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201128-15) It was sent to [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland") and took part in the [Battle of Glen Shiel](/wiki/Battle_of_Glen_Shiel "Battle of Glen Shiel") in June 1719 during [Jacobite rising](/wiki/Jacobite_rising_of_1715 "Jacobite rising of 1715").[[16]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201129-16)

The regiment was deployed to [South America](/wiki/South_America "South America"), where it took part in the [Battle of Cartagena de Indias](/wiki/Battle_of_Cartagena_de_Indias "Battle of Cartagena de Indias") in March 1741 during the [War of Jenkins' Ear](/wiki/War_of_Jenkins%27_Ear "War of Jenkins' Ear").[[17]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201132-17) It also saw action at the [Battle of Culloden](/wiki/Battle_of_Culloden "Battle of Culloden") in April 1746 during the next [Jacobite rising](/wiki/Jacobite_rising_of_1745 "Jacobite rising of 1745").[[18]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201134-18) In 1751, when the numerical system of designation of Regiments of Foot was adopted, it became the **15th Regiment of Foot**.[[4]](#cite_note-regiments-4)

The regiment went on to take part in [the capture](/wiki/Raid_on_Rochefort "Raid on Rochefort") of [Île-d'Aix](/wiki/%C3%8Ele-d%27Aix "Île-d'Aix") in 1757[[19]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201137-19) and, having sailed for [North America](/wiki/North_America "North America") in 1758,[[19]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201137-19) fought at the [Battle of the Plains of Abraham](/wiki/Battle_of_the_Plains_of_Abraham "Battle of the Plains of Abraham") in 1759 during the [Seven Years' War](/wiki/Seven_Years%27_War "Seven Years' War").[[20]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201142-20)

The regiment was sent to North America again in spring 1776 for service in the [American Revolutionary War](/wiki/American_Revolutionary_War "American Revolutionary War"). It saw action at the [Battle of Long Island](/wiki/Battle_of_Long_Island "Battle of Long Island") in August 1776, the [Battle of White Plains](/wiki/Battle_of_White_Plains "Battle of White Plains") in October 1776 and the [Battle of Fort Washington](/wiki/Battle_of_Fort_Washington "Battle of Fort Washington") in November 1776.[[21]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201148-21) It also took part in the [Battle of Brandywine](/wiki/Battle_of_Brandywine "Battle of Brandywine") in September 1777,[[22]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201150-22) the [Battle of Germantown](/wiki/Battle_of_Germantown "Battle of Germantown") in October[[23]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201151-23) and the [Battle of White Marsh](/wiki/Battle_of_White_Marsh "Battle of White Marsh") in December 1777.[[23]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201151-23)

In 1782 the regiment became the **15th (The Yorkshire East Riding) Regiment of Foot**.[[4]](#cite_note-regiments-4)

### Napoleonic Wars

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Yorkshire_Regiment&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Napoleonic Wars")]

The regiment was deployed to the [West Indies](/wiki/West_Indies "West Indies") in 1795 for service in the [French Revolutionary Wars](/wiki/French_Revolutionary_Wars "French Revolutionary Wars") and fought at attacks on [Martinique](/wiki/Martinique "Martinique") and [Guadeloupe](/wiki/Guadeloupe "Guadeloupe")[[24]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201157-24) before returning to England in 1796.[[25]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201158-25) The regiment returned to the West Indies in 1805[[26]](#cite_note-26) for service in the [Napoleonic Wars](/wiki/Napoleonic_Wars "Napoleonic Wars") and took part in the [invasion of Martinique](/wiki/Invasion_of_Martinique_(1809) "Invasion of Martinique (1809)") in January 1809 and the [invasion of Guadeloupe](/wiki/Invasion_of_Guadeloupe_(1810) "Invasion of Guadeloupe (1810)") in January 1810.[[27]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECannon201161-27)

### The Victorian era

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Yorkshire_Regiment&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: The Victorian era")]

The regiment spent most of the 19th century on garrison duty, both at home and throughout the [Empire](/wiki/British_Empire "British Empire"). The 1st Battalion was shipped to [New Brunswick](/wiki/New_Brunswick "New Brunswick") in 1862 at the time of the [Trent Affair](/wiki/Trent_Affair "Trent Affair"), when Britain and the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") came close to war. The regiment was not fundamentally affected by the [Cardwell Reforms](/wiki/Cardwell_Reforms "Cardwell Reforms") of the 1870s, which gave it a depot at [Victoria Barracks, Beverley](/wiki/Victoria_Barracks,_Beverley "Victoria Barracks, Beverley") from 1873, or by the [Childers reforms](/wiki/Childers_reforms "Childers reforms") of 1881 – as it already possessed two battalions, there was no need for it to amalgamate with another regiment.[[28]](#cite_note-28) Under the reforms the regiment became **The East Yorkshire Regiment** on 1 July 1881.[[29]](#cite_note-29) The 1st battalion was stationed at [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar") in 1885, moved to the West Indies in 1886, then to [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa") in 1888 and to [Egypt](/wiki/Egypt "Egypt") in 1893. From 1895 the battalion was stationed in [British India](/wiki/British_Raj "British Raj"), where they had various postings, including at [Belgaum](/wiki/Belgaum "Belgaum") and [Fort St. George](/wiki/Fort_St._George,_India "Fort St. George, India") in [Madras Presidency](/wiki/Madras_Presidency "Madras Presidency") until late 1902 when it was posted to [Shwebo](/wiki/Shwebo "Shwebo") in Burma.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

The 2nd Battalion was stationed in British India from 1875 to February 1888, and fought in the [Second Anglo-Afghan War](/wiki/Second_Anglo-Afghan_War "Second Anglo-Afghan War") (1879–80). After six months in [Aden](/wiki/Aden "Aden") that year, the battalion was back in England until November 1894, when it was stationed in Ireland.[[31]](#cite_note-31) The [Second Boer War](/wiki/Second_Boer_War "Second Boer War") started in South Africa in October 1899. After a series of defeats in the early months of the war, the British government sent large number of troops there as reinforcements in early 1900, including the 2nd Battalion East Yorkshire. 870 officers and men embarked on the SS *Nile* from [Southampton](/wiki/Southampton "Southampton") in March 1900, and arrived in South Africa the following month.[[32]](#cite_note-32)[[33]](#cite_note-33) The battalion stayed in South Africa throughout the war, returning home in late 1902 when they were stationed at [Aldershot](/wiki/Aldershot_Garrison "Aldershot Garrison").[[34]](#cite_note-34)

The 3rd ([Militia](/wiki/Militia_(United_Kingdom) "Militia (United Kingdom)")) battalion, formed from the [East York Militia](/wiki/East_York_Militia "East York Militia") in 1881, was a reserve battalion. It was embodied in May 1900, disembodied in December that year, and later re-embodied for service in South Africa during the Second Boer War.[[35]](#cite_note-35) About 560 officers and men returned to Southampton on the SS *Greek* in early October 1902, following the end of the war, when the battalion was disembodied at Beverley.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

In 1908, the Volunteers and Militia were reorganised nationally, with the former becoming the [Territorial Force](/wiki/Territorial_Force "Territorial Force") and the latter the [Special Reserve](/wiki/Special_Reserve_(militia) "Special Reserve (militia)");[[37]](#cite_note-37) the regiment now had one Reserve and two Territorial battalions.[[38]](#cite_note-38)[[4]](#cite_note-regiments-4)

### First World War

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Yorkshire_Regiment&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: First World War")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/47/NLS_Haig_-_Troops_moving_up_at_eventide_-_men_of_a_Yorkshire_regiment_on_the_march_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-NLS_Haig_-_Troops_moving_up_at_eventide_-_men_of_a_Yorkshire_regiment_on_the_march_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:NLS_Haig_-_Troops_moving_up_at_eventide_-_men_of_a_Yorkshire_regiment_on_the_march_(cropped).jpg)

#### Regular Army

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Yorkshire_Regiment&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Regular Army")]

The 1st Battalion landed at [Saint-Nazaire](/wiki/Saint-Nazaire "Saint-Nazaire") as part of the [18th Brigade](/wiki/18th_Infantry_Brigade_(United_Kingdom) "18th Infantry Brigade (United Kingdom)") in the [6th Division](/wiki/6th_Infantry_Division_(United_Kingdom) "6th Infantry Division (United Kingdom)") in September 1914 for service on the [Western Front](/wiki/Western_Front_(World_War_I) "Western Front (World War I)").[[39]](#cite_note-trail-39) The 2nd Battalion landed at [Le Havre](/wiki/Le_Havre "Le Havre") as part of the [83rd Brigade](/wiki/83rd_Brigade_(United_Kingdom) "83rd Brigade (United Kingdom)") in the [28th Division](/wiki/28th_Division_(United_Kingdom) "28th Division (United Kingdom)") in January 1915 also for service on the Western Front before moving to [Salonika](/wiki/Thessaloniki "Thessaloniki") in October 1915 for service on the [Macedonian front](/wiki/Macedonian_front "Macedonian front").[[39]](#cite_note-trail-39)

#### Territorial Force

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Yorkshire_Regiment&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Territorial Force")]

The [1/4th Battalion](/wiki/Hull_Rifles#1/4th_Battalion "Hull Rifles") landed at [Boulogne-sur-Mer](/wiki/Boulogne-sur-Mer "Boulogne-sur-Mer") as part of the [York and Durham Brigade](/wiki/150th_(York_and_Durham)_Brigade "150th (York and Durham) Brigade") in the [Northumbrian Division](/wiki/50th_(Northumbrian)_Division "50th (Northumbrian) Division") in April 1915 for service on the Western Front. The [2/4th Battalion](/wiki/Hull_Rifles#2/4th_Battalion "Hull Rifles") served in the [garrison](/wiki/Bermuda_Garrison "Bermuda Garrison") of the [Imperial fortress](/wiki/Imperial_fortress "Imperial fortress") [colony](/wiki/British_Overseas_Territory "British Overseas Territory") of [Bermuda](/wiki/Bermuda "Bermuda") (where the Right Wing, 15th Regiment of Foot had been stationed from 1819 to 1821, and the 1st Battalion, 15th Regiment of Foot had been stationed from 1868 to 1870) from November, 1916, through the end of the war,[[40]](#cite_note-40)[[41]](#cite_note-41)[[42]](#cite_note-42)[[43]](#cite_note-43) and the [3/4th Battalion](/wiki/Hull_Rifles#3/4th_Battalion "Hull Rifles") remained at home and trained reinforcements.[[39]](#cite_note-trail-39) The [5th (Cyclist) Battalion](/wiki/5th_(Cyclist)_Battalion,_East_Yorkshire_Regiment "5th (Cyclist) Battalion, East Yorkshire Regiment") served on coastal defence duties for the whole war.[[44]](#cite_note-44)

#### New Armies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Yorkshire_Regiment&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: New Armies")]

The 6th (Service) Battalion landed at [Suvla Bay](/wiki/Suvla "Suvla") in [Gallipoli](/wiki/Gallipoli "Gallipoli") as the pioneer battalion for the [11th (Northern) Division](/wiki/11th_(Northern)_Division "11th (Northern) Division") in August 1915; the battalion was evacuated in January 1916 and then landed at [Marseille](/wiki/Marseille "Marseille") in July 1916 for service on the Western Front.[[39]](#cite_note-trail-39) The 7th (Service) Battalion landed at Boulogne-sur-Mer as part of [50th Brigade](/wiki/50th_Brigade_(United_Kingdom) "50th Brigade (United Kingdom)") in the [17th (Northern) Division](/wiki/17th_(Northern)_Division "17th (Northern) Division") in July 1915 also for service on the Western Front.[[39]](#cite_note-trail-39) The [8th (Service) Battalion](/wiki/8th_(Service)_Battalion,_East_Yorkshire_Regiment "8th (Service) Battalion, East Yorkshire Regiment") landed at Boulogne-sur-Mer as part of the [62nd Brigade](/wiki/62nd_Brigade_(United_Kingdom) "62nd Brigade (United Kingdom)") in the [21st Division](/wiki/21st_Division_(United_Kingdom) "21st Division (United Kingdom)") in September 1915 also for service on the Western Front, later serving with [3rd Division](/wiki/3rd_(United_Kingdom)_Division "3rd (United Kingdom) Division"). The 9th (Reserve) Battalion remained at home supplying drafts to the New Army battalions serving overseas.[[39]](#cite_note-trail-39)

The [10th](/wiki/Hull_Pals "Hull Pals"), [11th](/wiki/Hull_Pals "Hull Pals"), [12th](/wiki/Hull_Pals "Hull Pals") and [13th (Service) Battalions](/wiki/Hull_Pals "Hull Pals") were raised in September 1914 from men volunteering in [Kingston upon Hull](/wiki/Kingston_upon_Hull "Kingston upon Hull"). These units were additionally entitled 1st, 2nd, 3rd and 4th City of Hull battalions and were known as the [Hull Pals](/wiki/Hull_Pals "Hull Pals"), nicknamed the 'Hull Commercials', 'Hull Tradesmen', 'Hull Sportsmen' and 'T'others' respectively. They formed [92nd Brigade](/wiki/92nd_Brigade_(United_Kingdom) "92nd Brigade (United Kingdom)") in [31st Division](/wiki/31st_Division_(United_Kingdom) "31st Division (United Kingdom)"), landed in [Egypt](/wiki/Egypt#British_protectorate_.281882.E2.80.931952.29 "Egypt") in December 1915 and then moved to France in March 1916 also for service on the Western Front. Their depot companies became the [14th (Reserve)](/wiki/Hull_Pals "Hull Pals") and [15th (Reserve) Battalions](/wiki/Hull_Pals "Hull Pals").[[39]](#cite_note-trail-39)[[45]](#cite_note-45)[[46]](#cite_note-46)

There were also a 1st Garrison Battalion that served in [India](/wiki/British_Raj "British Raj") and a 2nd (Home Service) Garrison Battalion.[[39]](#cite_note-trail-39)

### Between the wars

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Yorkshire_Regiment&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Between the wars")]

In 1935, the regiment was renamed **The East Yorkshire Regiment (The Duke of York's Own)**, after its Colonel-in-Chief.[[4]](#cite_note-regiments-4)

### Second World War

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Yorkshire_Regiment&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Second World War")]

The 1st Battalion was serving in [British India](/wiki/British_Raj "British Raj") on the outbreak of war in 1939 and did not see active service until 1942. In 1944, the Battalion was based in [Bethamangala](/wiki/Bethamangala "Bethamangala") as part of the [150th Indian Infantry Brigade](/wiki/150th_Indian_Infantry_Brigade "150th Indian Infantry Brigade"), where it was reorganised and trained in jungle warfare at the Jungle Warfare Training Center, Gudalur, in the [Mysore](/wiki/Mysore "Mysore") jungle and then played its part in the [Burma Campaign](/wiki/Burma_Campaign "Burma Campaign").[[47]](#cite_note-baf-47) It moved into Burma, crossing the [Chindwin River](/wiki/Chindwin_River "Chindwin River") at [Kalewa](/wiki/Kalewa "Kalewa") and the [Irrawaddy River](/wiki/Irrawaddy_River "Irrawaddy River") at Sameikkon in April 1945 and joined the [99th Indian Infantry Brigade](/wiki/99th_Mountain_Brigade "99th Mountain Brigade"), part of the [17th Indian Division](/wiki/17th_Infantry_Division_(India) "17th Infantry Division (India)") at [Meiktila](/wiki/Meiktila "Meiktila") after the siege by the Japanese had been broken.[[48]](#cite_note-48)

The 2nd Battalion served with the [8th Infantry Brigade](/wiki/8th_Infantry_Brigade_(United_Kingdom) "8th Infantry Brigade (United Kingdom)") (which included the 1st [Suffolks](/wiki/Suffolk_Regiment "Suffolk Regiment") and 1st [South Lancs](/wiki/South_Lancashire_Regiment "South Lancashire Regiment")), attached to the [3rd Infantry Division](/wiki/3rd_Division_(United_Kingdom) "3rd Division (United Kingdom)") throughout the whole war. At the time, the 3rd Division was commanded by [Major-General](/wiki/Major-general_(United_Kingdom) "Major-general (United Kingdom)") [Bernard Montgomery](/wiki/Bernard_Montgomery "Bernard Montgomery"), who would later command the Anglo-Canadian [21st Army Group](/wiki/21st_Army_Group "21st Army Group"). The battalion and division were sent to France in late 1939 as part of the [British Expeditionary Force](/wiki/British_Expeditionary_Force_(World_War_II) "British Expeditionary Force (World War II)") and remained there until May 1940 when they fought in the [Battle of France](/wiki/Battle_of_France "Battle of France") and were [evacuated at Dunkirk](/wiki/Dunkirk_evacuation "Dunkirk evacuation"). After Dunkirk, the battalion and division spent many years on home defence anticipating a [German invasion of England](/wiki/Operation_Sea_Lion "Operation Sea Lion"). After late 1942, when the threat of invasion had receded, they then started training for offensive operations and, in mid-1944, [invaded Normandy, France](/wiki/Normandy_landings "Normandy landings").[[47]](#cite_note-baf-47)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/00/The_British_Army_in_Normandy_1944_B7683.jpg/250px-The_British_Army_in_Normandy_1944_B7683.jpg)](/wiki/File:The_British_Army_in_Normandy_1944_B7683.jpg)

Men of the 2nd Battalion, East Yorkshire Regiment take cover behind a bank as an enemy shell explodes nearby, Normandy, France, 19 July 1944.

The [4th Battalion](/wiki/Hull_Rifles#4th_Battalion "Hull Rifles") was a 1st Line [Territorial Army](/wiki/Army_Reserve_(United_Kingdom) "Army Reserve (United Kingdom)") unit serving in the [150th Infantry Brigade](/wiki/150th_Infantry_Brigade_(United_Kingdom) "150th Infantry Brigade (United Kingdom)") in the [50th (Northumbrian) Infantry Division](/wiki/50th_(Northumbrian)_Infantry_Division "50th (Northumbrian) Infantry Division") and, like the 2nd Battalion, served in France 1940, were evacuated at Dunkirk to England and remained in the United Kingdom with the division until mid-1941 when it was sent to the [Middle East](/wiki/Mediterranean_and_Middle_East_theatre_of_World_War_II "Mediterranean and Middle East theatre of World War II"). It was captured at the [Battle of Gazala](/wiki/Battle_of_Gazala "Battle of Gazala").[[49]](#cite_note-49)

The [5th Battalion](/wiki/Hull_Rifles#5th_Battalion "Hull Rifles") was formed in June 1939 as a 2nd Line Territorial Army duplicate of the 4th Battalion. It served with the [69th Infantry Brigade](/wiki/69th_Infantry_Brigade_(United_Kingdom) "69th Infantry Brigade (United Kingdom)") in the [23rd (Northumbrian) Division](/wiki/23rd_(Northumbrian)_Division "23rd (Northumbrian) Division") and was evacuated from Dunkirk. It later served with 50th (Northumbrian) Division through the [Western Desert](/wiki/Western_Desert_campaign "Western Desert campaign"), [Tunisian](/wiki/Tunisian_Campaign "Tunisian Campaign") and [Sicily](/wiki/Allied_invasion_of_Sicily "Allied invasion of Sicily") campaigns before landing in the first wave on [Gold Beach](/wiki/Gold_Beach "Gold Beach") on [D Day](/wiki/D_Day "D Day") and then fighting through [Normandy](/wiki/Normandy_Campaign "Normandy Campaign") and [North West Europe](/wiki/Western_Front_(World_War_II)#1944–1945:_The_Second_Front "Western Front (World War II)").[[50]](#cite_note-50)

In the [Second World War](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), three hostilities-only battalions were raised. The 6th (Home Defence) Battalion, which was formed in November 1939, was redesignated as the 30th Battalion in December 1941 and disbanded in September 1943.[[51]](#cite_note-51)[[52]](#cite_note-52)

The 7th Battalion was formed in 1940 and assigned to 203 Infantry Brigade.[[53]](#cite_note-53) The battalion was transferred to 73 Infantry Brigade in July 1941 and to 162 Infantry Brigade in September 1942. It was disbanded in October 1944, having never left the UK.[[54]](#cite_note-54)

The 50th (Holding) Battalion was formed in May 1940.[[55]](#cite_note-55) In October, it was redesignated as the 8th battalion and assigned to 217 Infantry Brigade.[[56]](#cite_note-56)[[57]](#cite_note-57) In November 1941, the battalion was transferred to 224 Infantry Brigade, which was disbanded the following month. The battalion was then converted to [115 LAA Regiment, RA](/wiki/115th_Light_Anti-Aircraft_Regiment,_Royal_Artillery "115th Light Anti-Aircraft Regiment, Royal Artillery") and assigned to the [46th Infantry Division](/wiki/46th_Infantry_Division_(United_Kingdom) "46th Infantry Division (United Kingdom)"), with which it served in North Africa, Italy and Palestine.[[58]](#cite_note-58)[[59]](#cite_note-59)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/60/British_Forces_in_the_Middle_East%2C_1945-1947_E31759.jpg/250px-British_Forces_in_the_Middle_East%2C_1945-1947_E31759.jpg)](/wiki/File:British_Forces_in_the_Middle_East,_1945-1947_E31759.jpg)

Men of the 2nd Battalion, The East Yorkshire Regiment in a [Universal Carrier](/wiki/Universal_Carrier "Universal Carrier") on field exercise in Palestine

### After the War

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Yorkshire_Regiment&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: After the War")]

The regiment was in [Mandatory Palestine](/wiki/Mandatory_Palestine "Mandatory Palestine") during the [Zionist insurgency](/wiki/Mandatory_Palestine#Zionist_insurgency "Mandatory Palestine") and then took part in the [Malayan Emergency](/wiki/Malayan_Emergency "Malayan Emergency") in 1953–56 before returning to [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany") as part of the [British Army of the Rhine](/wiki/British_Army_of_the_Rhine "British Army of the Rhine"). In 1958, it was amalgamated with The West Yorkshire Regiment (The Prince of Wales's Own), to form the [Prince of Wales's Own Regiment of Yorkshire](/wiki/Prince_of_Wales%27s_Own_Regiment_of_Yorkshire "Prince of Wales's Own Regiment of Yorkshire").[[4]](#cite_note-regiments-4)

The regimental collection is held by the York Army Museum which is based at the [Tower Street drill hall](/wiki/Tower_Street_drill_hall,_York "Tower Street drill hall, York") in [York](/wiki/York "York").[[60]](#cite_note-60)

## The Snappers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Yorkshire_Regiment&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: The Snappers")]

The [London and North Eastern Railway](/wiki/London_and_North_Eastern_Railway "London and North Eastern Railway") named one of its V2 locomotives, No. 4780 (later No 809 and then British Railways 60809) as The Snapper. The East Yorkshire Regiment. The Duke of York's Own[[61]](#cite_note-61)
